decontamination_notes: Real bug fix from npm/node-semver PR #870, merged 2026-06-04, after model training cutoffs. SemVer#inc('prerelease', identifier) only compared the first prerelease component (this.prerelease[0]) against the identifier, so a DOTTED identifier (e.g. 'alpha.beta') never matched and the prerelease was reset instead of incrementing the numeric segment after the matched prefix. The fix (classes/semver.js) adds an isPrereleaseIdentifier helper that matches the dotted identifier against the leading prerelease components and increments the segment that follows. Repo sliced at the PR base commit (76416081); the whole node-semver library is included so require('./index') resolves. Pure JavaScript, zero external dependencies; hidden tests run under Node's built-in node:test (no image needed). ISC LICENSE preserved. Coverage floor: 3 fail_to_pass tests over dotted identifiers (partial prefix, full prefix, and a longer nested prefix), each of which resets the prerelease at base and increments correctly after the fix, plus a pass_to_pass regression guard (plain prerelease increment with no identifier, single-token identifier, and unrelated inc/valid ops) that holds at base and after the fix.

fail_to_pass.name: inc_dotted_alpha_beta
fail_to_pass.cmd: node --test --test-name-pattern='^vb inc dotted alpha beta$' vb_inc_prerelease.test.js

fail_to_pass.name: inc_dotted_alpha_beta_5
fail_to_pass.cmd: node --test --test-name-pattern='^vb inc dotted alpha beta 5$' vb_inc_prerelease.test.js

fail_to_pass.name: inc_dotted_alpha_10_beta
fail_to_pass.cmd: node --test --test-name-pattern='^vb inc dotted alpha 10 beta$' vb_inc_prerelease.test.js
